one_time_description: Additional COVID-19 funding received from Indigenous Services Canada and other sources for public health measures, income assistance, and reopening costs
economic_notes: Nation operates a confectionary/store (inventory includes confectionary and tobacco). Economic dependence on Western Cree Tribal Council and Indigenous Services Canada funding. COVID-19 pandemic impacted operations in 2021.
notes: Consolidated entity includes Duncan's Carmon Creek Community Trust. Demand loan of $1,397,035 classified as current liability (due on demand) despite repayment schedule to 2030. Wages split between general staff ($1,995,410) and Chief and Council ($262,063) totaling $2,257,473. No schedule of remuneration provided with individual amounts. COVID-19 additional funding of $1,247,322 received. Total government transfers calculated as WCTC $3,996,066 + ISC Health $1,029,738 + FNDF $371,049 + GoA $114,068 + CMHC $27,660 + Federal Trust resource revenue $26,564 = $5,165,145. OSR primarily from Other income. Restricted cash of $414,335 includes FNDF and CMHC Replacement Reserve held in general cash account.
